vocab_items.sentence: Tom was hungry, but he was lazy too.
vocab_items.explanation: Not wanting to work or use any effort.

vocab_items.sentence: He didn't want to go to a restaurant.
vocab_items.explanation: A place where you can buy and eat a meal.

vocab_items.sentence: He didn't even want to heat leftovers.
vocab_items.explanation: To make food warm or hot so it is ready to eat.

vocab_items.sentence: He didn't even want to heat leftovers.
vocab_items.explanation: Food that remains after a meal is finished.

vocab_items.sentence: He decided to get pizza delivered.
vocab_items.explanation: Brought to a person's house or place of work.
A button outside a house that you press to make a sound inside.
A person whose job is to bring goods or food to customers.
An extra amount of money given to someone for their service.

vocab_items.sentence: Tom sat back on the couch and ate the pizza.
vocab_items.explanation: A long, comfortable seat for two or more people; a sofa.
Having eaten enough food so that you do not feel hungry anymore.
Feeling pleased or happy because you have what you want or need.
To stay in one place until something happens or arrives.

vocab_items.sentence: At first, he was too lazy to get up.
vocab_items.explanation: To stand up after sitting or lying down.

vocab_items.sentence: Tom sat back on the couch and ate the pizza.
vocab_items.explanation: To relax in a chair and lean against its back.

vocab_items.sentence: Suddenly, there was a knock at the door again.
vocab_items.explanation: The sound of someone hitting a door to get attention.
To appear as if you want or need food.

vocab_items.sentence: Tom laughed and shared his pizza with the cat.
vocab_items.explanation: To give a part of something to someone else.
